response:
Sec. 1  
In 2019, the Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh was Chandrababu Naidu. He was the leader of the Telugu Desam Party. He served as the Chief Minister for several terms.  

***  
The state of Andhra Pradesh was formed in 1956. It was reorganized in 2014 to create a new state. Chandrababu Naidu was the first Chief Minister of the new state.  

***  
Chandrababu Naidu was born in 1952 in Kadapa district. He became a prominent political leader in the 1990s. He led the Telugu Desam Party to power in 1995.  

***  
He was re-elected as Chief Minister in 2004 and 2009. His government focused on economic development and infrastructure. He implemented various welfare schemes for the people.  

***  
In 2014, the state was divided into two parts, Andhra Pradesh and Telangana. Chandrababu Naidu continued to serve as the Chief Minister. His leadership was crucial during the reorganization.  

***  
He faced challenges during the transition period in 2014. His government worked to ensure stability and continuity. The people of Andhra Pradesh supported his leadership.  

***  
Chandrababu Naidu remained in power until 2019. His tenure was marked by significant political and economic changes. He was re-elected for the third time in 2019.  

***  
He was the longest-serving Chief Minister of Andhra Pradesh. His policies had a lasting impact on the state. He is remembered for his leadership and reforms.
